Records.description: Non-planar two-loop four-point family with one internal mass, relevant for $gg \to \gamma\gamma$ and $q\bar{q} \to \gamma\gamma$ via top quark. 4 massive and 3 massless propagators. Seven-propagator master integrals were previously unknown; IR-divergent sectors present.

Records.authors: Becchetti, Matteo, Bonciani, Roberto, Cieri, Leandro, Coro, Federico, Ripani, Federico
Records.description: Non-planar double-box integral family NPL for two-loop $q\bar{q} \to \gamma\gamma$ form factors with massive top quark loop. 36 master integrals divided into two subsets: (I) canonical logarithmic subset expressible in MPLs with a 30-letter alphabet containing 9 square roots, and (II) elliptic sectors containing 6 MIs. The elliptic sectors are: (a) a non-planar massive triangle (2 MIs, $g_{31}$-$g_{32}$, expressible in eMPLs) and (b) the top-sector non-planar double-box (4 MIs, $g_{33}$-$g_{36}$, original computation). The maximal cut of the double-box integral yields an elliptic curve $y_c^2 = (z_8+t)(z_8+s+t)(z_8-z_+)(z_8-z_-)$ which degenerates to the massive triangle curve in the forward limit $t=0$. System solved semi-analytically via DiffExp generalised power series. Boundary conditions: $g_1=f_1$, $g_2=f_2$, all others vanish at $y=z=0$. Numerical validation against AMFlow to 200 digits. Grid of 13752 phase-space points evaluated (2.5h PLA + 10.5h NPL on single core). Corresponds to diagram (b) in Fig. 1 where top quark couples to external photons in non-planar topology.
Records.location: Table 1 (propagator definitions), Section 3.1 (general setup), Section 3.3 (non-planar DEQs and elliptic analysis), Eqs. (20)-(22) (alphabet and roots), Appendix A (MI list: J17,J19,J20,J30-J34,J39-J42)

Records.authors: Ahmed, Taushif, Chaubey, Ekta, Kaur, Mandeep, Maggio, Sara
Records.description: Two-loop non-planar four-point integral family for NNLO QCD corrections to diphoton ($gg \to \gamma\gamma$) and dijet production at hadron colliders, with a massive top-quark loop (4 massive propagators, 3 massless). The family depends on three scales: $s$, $t$, and $m_t^2$. The top sector (7-propagator, integrals $J_{33}$-$J_{36}$) and the 6-propagator subsector ($J_{31}$-$J_{32}$) are elliptic, but the authors identify a single elliptic curve governing both sectors, contrary to earlier claims of two distinct curves. The paper derives an $\varepsilon$-factorized differential equation for the elliptic top sector via a sequence of transformations ($U_0$ through $U_4$), leveraging the factorization of the Picard-Fuchs operator and properties of the maximal cut. Analytic results are expressed as Chen's iterated integrals over elliptic one-forms involving periods of the elliptic curve. The ancillary file provides the full 36-MI basis enabling iterated-integral solutions at each order in $\varepsilon$.
Records.location: Eq. (2.2)-(2.3), Section 2; basis in Eq. (2.6); top-sector DE in Section 3 and Appendix A.1

Records.description: Non-planar two-loop 4-point family NPL2 for diphoton production with closed top loop. 36 MIs. One of the two non-planar families is elliptic; paper does not state which, so function_class='unknown'. Called `NPL2` in the paper.
Records.familyName: mixed-mass 2-loop 4-point non-planar double box (7 propagators, 4 massive)

Records.authors: Becchetti, Matteo, Bonciani, Roberto, Cieri, Leandro, Coro, Federico, Ripani, Federico
Records.description: Called 'NPL' in the paper. Non-planar double box family for two-loop QCD corrections to diphoton production (q qbar -> gamma gamma) with a closed massive top-quark loop. One massless sub-loop and one massive sub-loop (4 propagators with m_t^2) with non-planar routing. 36 master integrals. Contains elliptic sectors: a non-planar triangle with massive loop and the non-planar double-box top sector with 4 elliptic MIs. Canonical form only for the polylogarithmic subset (30-letter alphabet).
Records.location: Table 1 (propagator definitions), Section 3.1 (general setup), Section 3.3 (non-planar DEQs and elliptic analysis), Eqs. (20)-(22) (alphabet and roots), Appendix A (MI list: J17,J19,J20,J30-J34,J39-J42)

Names: mixed-mass 2-loop 4-point non-planar double box (7 propagators, 4 massive)
